mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing equity risk premiums to rise and S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during easing cycles do not predict positive 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230.0, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 230.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day forward returns in greater than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenues and profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 95.0, manufacturing contraction signals weakening economic activity, reducing corporate earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where INDPRO drops below 95.0.
